title: Turn Probe Opportunity
description: You're in the Big Blind with K♠ J♠.
The Cutoff opens to 2.5 BB. It folds to you; you call. Heads-up to the flop (pot 5.5 BB).
Flop (8♥ K♦ 7♥): you check, the Cutoff checks back.
Turn (3♠): the action is on you.
question: The pre-flop raiser declined to c-bet the flop. On the 3♠ turn, what is your play with K♠ J♠?
feedback: In theory all options are okay with this hand. In practice, a small bet is likely to underperform due to the lack of raising by population in this node. Population is likely quite capped and condensed when they check the flop here. Hands like 8x, 7x and 99, TT, JJ, QQ and weak top pair will appear at a higher frequency than in theory. Therefore B75 and B150 are the best options. X/B33 will also underperform due to lack of aggression across turn and river from the pool.
author: Pete Clarke
